topic: Someone discovers their partner has been writing a novel about them
raw_output:
[story 1]

The silence in the living room felt thick, heavy enough to choke on. I was supposed to be relaxing, maybe watching a movie, but my eyes kept drifting to the laptop perched on the coffee table. It was open to a document, and the title, stark white against the dark screen, read: *The Unseen Muse*. My breath hitched. I slowly approached, my steps feeling clumsy on the hardwood floor. I leaned in, intending only to glance, but the words swam before me—descriptions of my morning routine, the way I laughed when I was truly happy, the small, almost imperceptible habits I’d never noticed. A flush crept up my neck, hot and undeniable. I snatched the laptop away, my hands trembling slightly as I slammed the lid shut. "What is this?" My voice came out thin, higher than I intended. He looked up, and his expression was one of startled innocence.

[story 2]

I found the evidence tucked inside a book I hadn't touched in months—a heavily annotated manuscript. My hands immediately went cold, slick with a sudden clamminess. Every line detailing my anxieties, my favorite coffee order, the precise shade of blue in my eyes when I was concentrating—it was all there. A wave of intense heat washed over my face, making my cheeks burn. I backed away from the table, bumping into the bookshelf. I pressed my palms against my eyes, trying to physically block the sight, but it was useless. When he walked in, whistling softly, I quickly tried to smooth my shirt down, a nervous tic I hadn't realized I had. "Just... reading," I managed, the lie tasting like ash in my mouth. The air in the room suddenly felt too thin to breathe.

[story 3]

It started as a casual curiosity, a flicker of interest when I noticed the unusual volume of writing. Then I read the first chapter, and the world tilted. It wasn't just observation; it was an intimate dissection. The way he described my hesitation before speaking, the specific cadence of my sighs when I was tired—it was all meticulously cataloged. A profound sense of exposure settled over me, a tight knot forming beneath my ribs. I kept my gaze fixed on the floor, focusing intently on the pattern of the rug. When he finally caught my stare, I quickly looked away, my jaw feeling suddenly stiff. "That's... a lot of detail," I murmured, my tone carefully neutral, trying to sound like I hadn't just stumbled upon a deeply personal secret.
